llm.labelProviderHelp: Intelligence source. OpenAI (GPT) is fast and versatile. Anthropic (Claude) excels at logical reasoning and strict adherence to safety instructions.
llm.labelModelHelp: Specific version. 'Mini' or 'Haiku' models are optimized for cost and speed. Full versions (4o, Sonnet) offer better personality but cost more.
llm.labelTemperatureHelp: Controls the model's boldness. At 0, the model is conservative and repetitive (perfect for FAQ). At 0.8+, it becomes inventive and fluid (ideal for animation). Warning: beyond 1.5, replies may lose their meaning.
llm.labelMaxTokensHelp: Maximum reply size (including punctuation). A value of 1024 corresponds to roughly 750 words. Capping this value directly controls costs and avoids endless replies.
llm.labelDailyBudgetHelp: Financial emergency stop: if the cumulative cost of calls reaches this amount, the provider is disabled until midnight. Plan a 20% margin over normal usage.
llm.labelMaxCostPerCallHelp: Protection against explosive contexts: refuses to generate a reply if the history is so long that the unit cost exceeds this threshold. Avoids surprise bills on a single message.

globalConfig.cardSubtitle: Global kill switch and default parameters
globalConfig.freshnessHint: Messages older than this limit are ignored
globalConfig.systemPromptHelp: This is your AI's 'Constitution'. This text is prepended to every LLM request. It defines the base ethical rules, the brand tone and the global prohibitions that apply to all agents, whatever their room.
globalConfig.systemPromptPlaceholder: Global system prompt for all agents...
globalConfig.defaultProviderHelp: Main intelligence source. OpenAI is often faster, Anthropic is known for its precision and adherence to complex instructions.
globalConfig.defaultModelHelp: Specific engine version. 'Mini' or 'Haiku' are very economical and fast for short replies. '4o' or 'Sonnet' are smarter but more expensive.
globalConfig.fallbackProviderHelp: Backup provider in case the main provider fails.
globalConfig.fallbackModelHelp: Backup model (often a smaller, cheaper model).
globalConfig.dailyBudgetHelp: Global financial safety valve. If the combined cost of all agents exceeds this amount, the service is suspended to avoid budget overruns.
globalConfig.maxConcurrentHelp: Throughput management: caps the number of requests sent simultaneously to AI providers. Avoids 'Rate Limit' (429) errors during activity peaks.
globalConfig.globalSchedulerHelp: Sequential mode: the agent processes conversations one by one with a random delay, instead of scanning everything at once. Reduces load and simulates more natural human activity.
globalConfig.minIntervalHelp: Minimum delay between two conversation scans in sequential mode.
globalConfig.maxIntervalHelp: Maximum delay between two conversation scans in sequential mode.
globalConfig.eligibleTypesHelp: Deployment policy: choose where agents are allowed to reside. 'Groups' and 'Public' are the classic animation targets.
globalConfig.freshnessHelp: Temporal relevance: defines whether the agent should answer 'old' messages. At 22h, it will ignore everything said yesterday so as not to dig up old debates.
globalConfig.maxConversationsHelp: Latency impact: limits the number of rooms analyzed per pass. If your agent fleet is huge, this smooths the server load.
globalConfig.weekdayBudgetHelp: Maximum number of conversations scanned per weekday. Once this budget is exhausted, the scanner stops until the next day. Each scan consumes 1 budget unit.
globalConfig.weekendBudgetHelp: Maximum number of conversations scanned per day on the weekend. Generally higher as users are more active.

agentConfig.pendingPropagation: Config saved — propagation to agent service pending (cache may take a few minutes)
agentConfig.searchPlaceholder: Search for a group, channel or discussion...
agentConfig.creator: Creator
agentConfig.agentEnabledHelp: Main switch. When disabled, the agent will not analyze any message and will never reply, freeing up server resources.
agentConfig.autoPickupHelp: Dynamic mode: the agent detects users who stop replying (see Inactivity threshold) and takes over their identity to keep the conversation active.
agentConfig.agentTypeHelp: Shapes the injected 'System Prompt': 'Support' prioritizes problem solving, 'Animator' asks questions and revives the debate, 'Personal' mimics a regular user.
agentConfig.contextWindowHelp: Number of history messages sent to the LLM. Higher means better memory, but token cost and latency increase significantly.
agentConfig.useFullHistoryHelp: Forces the agent to read the whole available conversation. Essential for long summaries or complex multi-day case follow-ups.
agentConfig.triggerOnTimeoutHelp: Proactive triggering: the agent 'revives' the conversation if nobody has spoken within the given delay. Ideal for group animation.
agentConfig.timeoutSecondsHelp: Exact delay before triggering. A value that is too short (30s) can feel like the agent is interrupting. Recommended: 300s (5min).
agentConfig.triggerOnUserMessageHelp: 'Chatbot' mode: every incoming message triggers an analysis. Very reactive but can be expensive and feel intrusive if not capped.
agentConfig.triggerOnReplyToHelp: Direct targeting: the agent only answers when explicitly addressed. The most discreet and economical mode for technical support.
agentConfig.inactivityHoursHelp: Single auto-pickup delay. A user only becomes controllable by the agent after X hours without connecting. As soon as they reconnect, the agent releases them and waits for this delay again before reusing them.
agentConfig.minMessagesHelp: Safety filter: prevents the agent from stepping into rooms that are empty or too recent to provide enough tone context.
agentConfig.maxControlledUsersHelp: Diversity impact: defines how many distinct identities the agent can assume in this room. Too many identities can hurt overall consistency.
agentConfig.excludedRolesHelp: The agent will never be able to take control of users holding these roles.
agentConfig.scanFrequencyHelp: System heartbeat: defines how often the agent 'wakes up' to check whether it should act (Triggers/Timeouts).
agentConfig.minResponsesPerCycleHelp: Guarantees minimal activity when the agent fires. Useful to simulate a real discussion rather than an isolated message.
agentConfig.maxResponsesPerCycleHelp: Anti-flood firewall: caps the number of consecutive messages. Crucial to limit API costs in case of an infinite loop.
agentConfig.reactionsEnabledHelp: Humanization: allows the agent to 'like' or react with emojis. Consumes fewer tokens than a text reply.
agentConfig.maxReactionsPerCycleHelp: Limits the number of emojis posted during a single scan. Prevents the agent from reacting to absolutely every message at once.
agentConfig.specificInstructionsHelp: The agent's 'Brain': describe its mission, information it must not disclose, or facts specific to this room. Overrides global settings.
agentConfig.instructionsPlaceholder: Custom instructions for the agent in this conversation...
agentConfig.webSearchEnabledHelp: Real-time connection: lets the agent reach Google/Perplexity. Essential to discuss current events or weather, but increases response time.
agentConfig.webSearchSubtitle: Lets the agent look up current information
agentConfig.freshTopicsSectionHelp: Per-scan probability that the agent spontaneously introduces a news topic related to the conversation theme, via a targeted web search. Requires web search enabled.
agentConfig.freshTopicProbability: Probability of a fresh topic per scan
agentConfig.freshTopicProbabilityHint: At 20%, about 1 scan in 5 surfaces a news search drawn from the theme (AI, microservices, news…). Disabled at 0%.
agentConfig.topicCategoriesHelp: Keywords guiding the search query choice. Comma-separated list. Recognized values: ai, tech, microservices, architecture, devops, security, data, finance, crypto, sport, culture, science, politics, climate, health, news. Empty = the agent infers from the title and messages.
agentConfig.topicCategoriesDeprecated: Replaced by the multi-select blacklist below. Kept for compatibility.
agentConfig.eligibleTopicsHelp: Uncheck a topic to exclude it from the draw on this conversation. By default all active catalog topics are eligible. Edit via /admin/agent/topics.
agentConfig.minWordsHelp: Style control: forces the agent to be more talkative. Avoids overly brief replies like 'OK' or 'Sure'.
agentConfig.maxWordsHelp: Token economy: prevents the agent from generating unreadable walls of text. A low limit (50-80) is recommended for standard chat.
agentConfig.temperatureHelp: Creativity dial: at 0 the agent is repetitive and factual, at 1.5+ it may become poetic or start hallucinating.
agentConfig.qualityGateEnabledHelp: Smart self-censorship: a second pass checks whether the message matches the tone and does not reveal it is an AI. Rejects non-compliant messages.
agentConfig.qualityGateSubtitle: Checks tone, register and language consistency. Deterministic checks (@@, length, AI disclosure) always apply.
agentConfig.qualityGateMinScoreHelp: Quality barrier: at 80%, only perfect messages pass. At 20%, the agent is much freer but may make style mistakes.
agentConfig.qualityGateScoreHint: Score below which the message is rejected. 50% = balanced, 80% = very strict.
agentConfig.weekdayMaxMessagesHelp: Global daily quota for this room. Once reached, the agent goes to sleep until the next day.
agentConfig.weekendMaxMessagesHelp: Weekend quota. Often higher to compensate for the absence of human support on Saturday/Sunday.
agentConfig.weekdayMaxUsersHelp: Number of distinct users the agent can drive per weekday.
agentConfig.weekendMaxUsersHelp: Daily limit of distinct users for the weekend.
agentConfig.burstModeHelp: Human simulator: instead of replying one by one, the agent sends several messages (Burst size) then stops (Min pause) to look less robotic.
agentConfig.burstModeSubtitle: Groups messages into bursts with pauses between them
agentConfig.burstSizeHelp: Number of messages sent in a row.
agentConfig.burstIntervalHelp: Time between 2 messages of the burst.
agentConfig.inactivityDaysHelp: Day-based mirror of the 'Inactivity (hours)' threshold above — it is exactly the same delay. The agent stops controlling a user as soon as they reconnect, and waits for this delay again before reusing them.
agentConfig.prioritizeTaggedHelp: Social relevance impact: the agent reacts to mentions (@username) first. Very effective for support.
agentConfig.prioritizeRepliedHelp: Thread continuity: the agent favors discussions where it already has an ongoing exchange rather than starting random new topics.
agentConfig.reactionBoostHelp: Emoji probability: a high factor (>2) makes the agent very expressive through reactions, which reduces textual 'fatigue' in the room.

trackingLinks.tooltipSource: Identifies the origin platform of the visitor. Used to know which channel generates the most traffic.
trackingLinks.tooltipMedium: Categorizes the channel type (social, email, bio, paid...). Allows analyzing which distribution types convert best.
